title: RECOVERY MENTORING - 211info
text: TRUE COLORS RECOVERY RECOVERY MENTORING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides substance use recovery peer services led by professionals with lived experience, who help participants create a personalized recovery pathway. The program also offers a community center where individuals can access mutual aid groups, on-site STI and Hepatitis C testing, skill-building opportunities, and resource fairs. 3807 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who identify LGBTQ2+ and have a substance use disorder Hours: 7 days per week 2pm-10pm Email (971) 252-1943 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (971) 252-1943 Community Center Front Desk Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in, call, or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties SITE HOURS: 2pm-10pm Services: LGBTQ2+ Community Centers * Substance Use Disorder Issues Peer Mental Health Support Services * LGBTQ2+ Issues Sexually Transmitted Infection Screening * LGBTQ2+ Issues If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
